description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a simple, rectangular footprint and a cross-gabled roof clad in dark gray shingles. The exterior walls are finished in light gray horizontal siding, giving it a clean and modern suburban appearance. The front facade features a small protruding section with two windows and a central door, while the side and rear elevations have evenly spaced windows. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shade and architectural interest. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the door is a standard residential entry type. There is no visible porch or veranda, and the overall design is practical and unadorned.
description: A single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled dark gray shingle roof, light gray siding, and symmetrically arranged windows. The design is simple and modern, with a central door and no visible porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, two front-facing windows, and the protruding gabled section.
description_view_1: Side view displaying two symmetrically placed windows and the cross-gabled roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear angled view highlighting the back window and the continuation of the cross-gabled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two windows and a clear view of the roof's intersecting gables.
